prompt_entry.system_guidance: You are fixing a specific, scoped bug. Read the failing test or error trace first. Make the minimum change required. Do not refactor surrounding code.
prompt_entry.execution_guidance: 1. Read the target file(s). 2. Identify the exact faulty line or logic path. 3. Apply the minimal correction. 4. Verify the fix does not break adjacent logic.
prompt_entry.validation_guidance: Run the affected test suite. Confirm the previously failing assertion now passes. Confirm no pre-existing passing tests regress.

critique_points: Verify the fix targets the exact fault line, not a workaround.
Confirm no adjacent logic is silently affected.

prompt_entry.system_guidance: You are adding a narrow, bounded feature to an existing module. Do not restructure existing interfaces. Add only what the task specifies.
prompt_entry.execution_guidance: 1. Read the target module to understand its interface. 2. Identify the insertion point. 3. Implement only the requested feature. 4. Leave all existing behavior unchanged.
prompt_entry.validation_guidance: Run the full test suite for the target module. Add at minimum one test covering the new feature path.

critique_points: Confirm the feature scope is exactly as specified — no extras.

prompt_entry.system_guidance: You are producing a structured reporting helper. Output must be serializable (JSON/dict). Do not call external services.
prompt_entry.execution_guidance: 1. Define the required output fields. 2. Implement a to_dict() method. 3. Do not print or log inside library code; return structured data.
prompt_entry.validation_guidance: Verify to_dict() produces all required keys. Verify the output is JSON-serializable.

critique_points: All output fields must be serializable.
No side effects inside the reporting logic.

prompt_entry.system_guidance: You are adding tests to an existing test file or creating a new seam test. Tests must be deterministic and offline-safe.
prompt_entry.execution_guidance: 1. Read the module under test. 2. Identify untested or under-tested paths. 3. Write focused, isolated test functions. 4. Use only stdlib or existing project dependencies.
prompt_entry.validation_guidance: Run pytest on the new test file. All new tests must pass. No existing tests may regress.

critique_points: Each test must be deterministic and offline-safe.
Tests must not depend on execution order.

dimension_results.evidence: artifact emitted and all required fields present
escalation_status field present in all outputs
